epigraph.text: Democracy is the theory that the common people know what they want, and deserve to get it good and hard.

follyOfTheDay.headline: HOA Fines Resident $1000/Day for Taupe Betrayal
follyOfTheDay.summary: What began as a simple desire to repaint one's domicile in beige—a color roughly as adventurous as oatmeal—has blossomed into a monetary hemorrhage of apocalyptic proportions. A homeowners' association in an undisclosed subdivision has levied fines of one thousand dollars per diem upon a hapless resident whose choice of exterior paint strayed from the officially sanctioned taupe. The resident, faced with geometrically increasing debt and armed only with the knowledge that beige and taupe are, to the human eye, essentially indistinguishable, has taken their tormentors to court. This represents the apotheosis of suburban governance: the transformation of paint swatches into instruments of financial ruin. The HOA's response remains unmoved—their bylaws, they insist, are unambiguous, however much natural human perception might suggest otherwise.
follyOfTheDay.commentary: When homeowners' associations begin waging economic warfare over paint nomenclature, one must wonder if democracy itself has simply lost interest in the proceedings.
